# Redemption regret calculator

: Ranjan kumar Singh
Created time: March 26, 2024 5:37 PM
Status: In progress
Last edited: February 19, 2026 7:15 PM
Owner: Lalit Bihani

# **What problem are we solving?**

- Our TG sell their assets to meet short-term cash needs, unaware that they can leverage their assets to achieve their short-term goals more effectively.
- Others explore alternatives to meet short-term need such as personal loans or business loans, but often encounter challenges such as high interest rates & other charges, cumbersome application processes, closure of loan.
- Some are hesitant to take loans due to a lack of understanding between good loans and bad loans and end up selling assets to meet goal.
- Currently,  MFDs rely on pen and paper to explain to their clients the benefits of LAMF and the potential losses associated with selling mutual funds.
- Through RRC, we aim to address the following objectives:
    - Education and awareness about LAMF to out TG
    - Branding through marketing and organic sharing

---

---

User query: [Plan to answer all such query on internet after launch]

[https://www.quora.com/How-do-I-calculate-profit-for-tax-calculation-when-mutual-fund-partial-withdrawal-redemption-is-done](https://www.quora.com/How-do-I-calculate-profit-for-tax-calculation-when-mutual-fund-partial-withdrawal-redemption-is-done)

---

# **What is the solution?**

## Requirements overview (optional)

- Educate our TG about why they should choose LAMF for short term cash need instead of selling mutual funds through calculator.
    - Showcase potential of LAMF
    - Show gain/profit with LAMF and losses on MF sell
- To achieve above, we should:
    - Create a **multi-lingual** RRC landing page with the option to share the page or output on DM or social media platforms.
    - Plug RRC on
        - Main landing page [ No API required here]
        - MFC fetch landing page [Pre-fill average return, Available cash, CGST & SGST]
        - App journey? [in case of drop-off?]
        - MFD dashboard

## User stories / User flow

As a user:

1. Upon landing on the Volt Money landing page, I want to see a dedicated section that provides clear information on why Loan Against Mutual Funds (LAMF) is the best option for me if I need cash urgently.
2. I want to understand the benefits of choosing LAMF over selling my mutual funds.
3. I need to know how much my portfolio will grow at X ROI if I choose not to sell my mutual funds and total net gain after X years.
4. If I opt for LAMF, I want to know the total cost of the loan, the repayment cycle(Plug OD calculator here), and the monthly and yearly EMI amounts.
5. I want to understand the potential losses in terms of long-term return on investment and the amount of tax I would need to pay if i redeem/sell MF.
6. I should be able to compare LAMF with other types of loans such as Personal Loans (PL), business loans, or car loans, in order to make an informed decision.
7. Finally, I need to comprehend the procedure for LAMF, including information on interest rates and charges, how repayment works, how withdrawals are processed, how pledging works, and how Loan to Value (LTV) ratios are calculated.
8. I want to see use case for which i can use LAMF OD, if not immediate requirement
9. I want to see actual user feedback/testimonial  
10. I want want to know whether Volt app is available on Android/iOS 

Point no 7 to 10 are applicable only for RRC landing page

# **Appendix**

## Meeting notes

- GTM/marketing : user withdraig MF, stop him and show his loss and then show gain with LAMF

Insight based on user call

User who have taken loan with us understand that

- LAMF is best option in case of short term requirement
- Monetary Gain & loss if they take LAMF vs if they sell MF - How they calculate monetary gain
- Benefits of LAMF over other loan like document required for PL, car loan, business loan.
- They will not get units at same NAV if they sell and than reinvest in MF
- Users who invest through SIP understand that it takes time to accumulate savings, and they recognize that breaking their savings for short-term needs may not be beneficial.
- LAMF is considered a favorable loan option, unlike Personal Loans
- Product benefits like: withdraw anytime when required, flexible repayment, interest based on usage, OD can be used in case of emergency cash requirement.
- MF will grow, will remain intact which will help to achieve long term goal
- Tax benefits if they do not sell MF
- Dividend gain to also consider as additional gain
- To enhance trust, we should showcase partnerships with TATA and BAJAJ. As one user pointed out, while not everyone may have heard of Volt, TATA's renowned reputation adds an extra layer of credibility that can resonate with a broader audience.
